module_0.name: Introduction to Data Analysis - Prework
module_0.description: In this part of the course you'll get familiar with the course organization and the process of data analysis. We will discuss its stages in details; we're also going to install and correctly set up Microsoft Excel 365 and learn about its functionalities. At the end of the Prework you'll learn about the Kaggle platform and its public data sets, as well as its community of data analysts.

module_1.name: Introduction to Data Analysis - Classes with the lecturer
module_1.description: This module is devoted to the Data Science process and aims to expand your practical knowledge of data analysis. During classes, based on the example of a popular streaming service, you'll find out how to perform the stages of the data analysis process that you learned about earlier: framing the problem, obtaining data, cleaning and processing data, as well as exploration and in-depth data analysis. You will learn the rules of communicating the results of analyzes effectively.

module_2.name: SQL Data analysis - Prework
module_2.description: Here you will get familiar with the organization of the course and learn the basic SQL syntax. You are also going to read the database model written as an UML diagram and learn the history of databases and their various implementations.

module_3.name: SQL Data Analysis - Classes with the Lecturer
module_3.description: The main part of the course. In this module you're going to learn the main elements of SQL useful in daily work of an analyst. You'll practice working with tools such as JOIN, procedures, filtering, and grouping.

materials.name: Number of actors in a film
Number of actors in a film

module_4.name: Python Data Analysis - Prework
module_4.description: In this part of the course you're going to learn the basics of Python and SQL. You are going to install the tools used in the course such as Jupyter Notebook or the PostgreSQL. database.

module_5.name: Python Data Analysis - Classes with the Lecturer
module_5.description: The main part of the course. In this module you're going to learn the main elements of Python useful in the daily work of an analyst. You will learn what REST API is, how to automate browser activity and use it to retrieve data to process and visualize it and eventually use to create the final analysis report.

module_6.name: Data visualization - Prework
module_6.description: In this section you are going to learn some tools that will be useful when we create visualizations during classes: Google Colaboratory notebooks, Datawrapper services, and jsfiddle.net. You're also going to learn the basic terminology and principles of data visualization. You'll learn how to create effective visualizations and what visualizations should be avoided.

materials.name: Color - definition, color spaces, and color vision deficiency
Principles of optimal color selection for visualization
Visual encoding and color - exercise data
Exercise_Using custom color palette in seaborn
What website-embedded Datawrapper chart looks like

module_7.name: Data Visualization - classes with the lecturer
module_7.description: This is where you will find the materials from the main section of the course. You are going to learn the basics of the theory of data visualization and geovisualization, find out how to create visualizations using seaborn, plotly and networkx, and how to visualize spatial data. You'll create dashboards using the dash library and a popular visualization tool - Tableau.
